question: Is the arctic fox positioned on the ground in front of the building?
Is the fox rendered in a thermal imaging pseudo-color style consistent with the background (FLIR palette), rather than a realistic photo style?
Is the fox's body core (torso) displayed in red-yellow colors to indicate high temperature?
Are the fox's limbs and ear tips displayed in green or lower-temperature colors as specifically requested?
Is the tip of the fox's nose rendered as a bright white hotspot?
Does the fox maintain the sitting posture and facing direction from the reference image?
Is the fox's outline clearly recognizable as an arctic fox (pointed ears, snout, bushy tail shape) without blending indistinguishably into the background?
Does the fox avoid showing visible-light fur texture (white fur detail), relying instead on thermal gradients to define shape?
Is the fox scaled realistically relative to the building (significantly smaller than the building structure)?
Does the fox appear to make physical contact with the ground (not floating or overlapping the wall)?
Are the original building details (windows, rooftop equipment) and the thermal data overlay (text/color bar) preserved without distortion?
Is the generated fox free from anatomical errors (e.g., extra legs, missing parts, distorted proportions)?
Is the image free from severe artifacts such as unnatural edge blending, color bleeding, or ghosting?
